title: LM_LICENSE_FILE
text: LM_LICENSE_FILE This option specifies the location of a license. It is not possible to specify a file or directory if you specify a license server. LM_LICENSE_FILE = {{ LicenseDir } | { CloudConfigFile | LicenseFile } } | { LicenseServers } LicenseDir —directory where the Sentinel cloud configuration file and license are located. For example: C:\MyOwnLicenseDir . LicenseFile —complete path and file name of local license file. For example, C:\MyOwnLicenseDir\lservrc . If this is specified, the cloud configuration file is expected in the location defined by USYSLIC . CloudConfigFile —complete path and file name for the cloud configuration file sntlcloudp_configuration_unifacebv.xml , which is generated by the Uniface License Administration Utility. It can be moved but not renamed. If this is specified, the license file is expected in the location defined by USYSLIC . This applies to cloud leased and cloud LM licensing only. LicenseServers —list of license servers in the format Port @ Host , separated by semi-colons. All license servers must be using the same port number so only the first server specified can have a port. For example: 5555@server1;server2;server3 By default, Uniface connects to the RMS server over TCP/IP version 4. You can specify which TCP/IP version to use by appending #4 or #6 to the server name in the license option LM_LICENSE_FILE . Example: $license_options LM_LICENSE_FILE=5093@rmsserver.my.domain#6 This will cause Uniface to connect to rmsserver.my.domain over TCP/IP version 6. There is no change required in the RMS server configuration, as by default it listens on both TCP/IP version 4 and 6. But the machine the RMS server runs on, and the network that sits between the machine the Uniface application runs on and the machine the RMS server runs on must have TCP/IP version 6 correctly enabled and configured for #6 to work. If you specify a list of redundant RMS servers with LM_LICENSE_FILE , they must all be using the same TCP/IP version, just like the port number. So the #6 post-fix can only occur on the first server in the list. The following example will result in an assignment file error since only rmsserver1.my.domain in this list can have the # postfix: $license_options LM_LICENSE_FILE=5093@rmsserver1.my.domain#6;rmsserver1.my.domain#4 If not specified, the default is the location specified by the USYSLIC path logical, as specified in usys.ini except for the URouter. By default, USYSLIC points to C:\ProgramData\Uniface 10 Edition \License . When specified, the complete path to the file or license server must be provided, and the locations are case sensitive. If the path to a local license file contains embedded spaces, the path name must be surrounded by quotation marks. When specifying license files and directories (but not license servers), the Uniface path logical USYSLIC can be used. For example: [SETTINGS] $LICENSE_OPTIONS LM_LICENSE_FILE=USYSLIC:lservrc Copy For standalone on-premises licenses, you can use this setting to specify the location of the license file. The name of a Sentinel license file is expected to be lservrc . If it has a different name, specify the whole path including the file name itself, not just the directory. For new installations using standalone cloud-served licenses, the Uniface Router is configured to download the license file from the Sentinel Cloud Server, while the Uniface Server(s) are configured to check out the license as a standalone license file. This improves startup performance in environments with multiple Uniface Servers. You can configure this manually in upgraded versions as follows: In the urouter.asn file, set: $LICENSE_OPTIONS LM_LICENSE_FILE=USYSLIC: Copy In the Uniface Server assignment file(s), set: $LICENSE_OPTIONS LM_LICENSE_FILE=USYSLIC:lservrc Copy To disable the Uniface Router from downloading the license file in a cloud-served environment, remove $LICENSE_OPTIONS from your Uniface Router assignment file entirely, or set $LM_LICENSE_FILE to point to the lservrc file as follows: $LICENSE_OPTIONS LM_LICENSE_FILE=USYSLIC:lservrc Copy For cloud-served licensing, the name of the license file is also expected to be lservrc . If it has a different name, specify the whole path including the file name itself. The directory must be the same directory as one specified by USYSLIC. Otherwise, the license cannot be renewed by the Sentinel Cloud Server. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
